summary:
Apex Trader Funding scored 74/100 in our 2026 review. The firm has transitioned from legacy monthly subscription evaluations to one-time fee products with 30-day access and no recurring billing. Current products are split by drawdown model — EOD (trailing End-of-Day drawdown with Daily Loss Limit) and Intraday (trailing real-time drawdown with no DLL) — with vendor options including Rithmic, Tradovate, and WealthCharts.

Account sizes available for new purchases are 25K, 50K, 100K, and 150K. Evaluations have no minimum trading days and can be passed in as little as 1 day. Funded Performance Accounts (PAs) use tier-based contract scaling. Payout eligibility requires 5 qualifying trading days with minimum daily profit thresholds. Approved payouts are paid at a 100% split. Safety net is required for the life of the PA, with only profit above the safety net eligible for withdrawal. Maximum 6 payout requests per PA with a $500 minimum and 50% payout consistency rule.

Apex accepts traders from over 100 countries with international payouts supported through Plane (ACH for US traders). The firm offers futures across equity, currency, agricultural, energy, crypto (micro only), and EUREX, though COMEX and all metals are temporarily unavailable. Trading platforms include Rithmic, Tradovate, WealthCharts, NinjaTrader (for Rithmic setup), and RTrader Pro.
pros: One-time evaluation fees with no recurring billing
100% approved payout split on funded accounts
No minimum trading days to pass evaluation
Multiple vendor options: Rithmic, Tradovate, WealthCharts
Two drawdown models (EOD and Intraday) for different trading styles
cons: Safety net required for life of PA — limits withdrawable profit
50% payout consistency rule restricts payout eligibility
COMEX and all metals temporarily unavailable
No resets on current evaluation products
PA activation must be completed within 7 calendar days
Automated systems, copy trading, and news trading rules not clearly confirmed
bestFor: Experienced futures traders who want one-time evaluation fees, 100% payout split, flexible drawdown models (EOD vs Intraday), and access to multiple vendor platforms
notIdealFor: Traders who need metals/COMEX access, those who want more than 6 payouts per account, beginners seeking structured education, or traders who require confirmed rules on automation and copy trading

verdictBlocks.heading: Our Final Verdict On Apex Trader Funding
verdictBlocks.body:
Apex Trader Funding scored 74/100 in our 2026 review. The firm has overhauled its product offering — current evaluations use one-time fees with 30-day access and no recurring billing, replacing the legacy monthly subscription model. Two drawdown models are available: EOD (trailing End-of-Day with Daily Loss Limit) and Intraday (trailing real-time with no DLL), with vendor options of Rithmic, Tradovate, and WealthCharts.

Current account sizes are 25K, 50K, 100K, and 150K. Evaluations have no minimum trading days and can be passed in a single day. Funded Performance Accounts use tier-based contract scaling. Approved payouts are paid at 100% split, with eligibility based on 5 qualifying trading days, minimum daily profit thresholds, a $500 minimum payout, and a 50% consistency rule. The safety net is required for the life of the PA, and there is a hard cap of 6 payout requests per PA.

Apex accepts traders from over 100 countries, with US payouts via ACH and international payouts through Plane. The platform stack centres on Rithmic, Tradovate, and WealthCharts, with NinjaTrader available for Rithmic setup. Futures markets cover equity, currency, agricultural, energy, crypto (micro only), and EUREX — though COMEX and all metals are temporarily unavailable. Support includes a Help Center, FAQs, guides, support tickets, master training courses, platform guides, and webinars. Legacy products (250K, 300K, Static accounts, monthly subscriptions) are no longer available for new purchases.

faq.a: Apex Trading refers to the evaluation and funded account programs offered by Apex Trader Funding, a futures prop firm. Traders qualify for funded Performance Accounts by meeting profit targets on one-time fee evaluations using platforms like Rithmic, Tradovate, or WealthCharts. Current account sizes are 25K, 50K, 100K, and 150K, with approved payouts paid at a 100% split.

Yes. Approved payouts are paid at a 100% split. Payout eligibility requires 5 qualifying trading days with minimum daily profit thresholds. The safety net is required for the life of the PA, and only profit above the safety net is eligible. Maximum 6 payout requests per PA with a $500 minimum and 50% payout consistency rule. US payouts via ACH, international payouts through Plane.

faq.q: Is Apex Trader Funding a scam or legit?
faq.a: No, Apex Trader Funding is not a scam and is considered a legit prop trading firm, with a TrustPilot rating of 4.4/5 based on over 16,900 reviews. The firm accepts traders from 100+ countries and has an active Discord community of 59,000+ members.

faq.q: What are the current Apex Trader Funding products?
faq.a: Current products use one-time evaluation fees with 30-day access and no recurring billing. Two drawdown models are available: EOD (trailing End-of-Day drawdown with Daily Loss Limit) and Intraday (trailing real-time drawdown with no DLL). Vendor options include Rithmic, Tradovate, and WealthCharts. Account sizes are 25K, 50K, 100K, and 150K. Legacy products (250K, 300K, Static accounts, monthly subscriptions) are no longer available.

faq.q: What are the Apex Trader Funding account sizes?
faq.a: Current account sizes available for new purchases are 25K, 50K, 100K, and 150K. Legacy sizes of 250K and 300K are no longer available. Each size has specific profit targets, drawdown structures, and contract limits that vary by drawdown model (EOD vs Intraday).

faq.q: How many trading days to pass the Apex evaluation?
faq.a: Current evaluations have no minimum trading days to pass. Traders can pass in as little as 1 day. Evaluations last 30 days with no recurring billing. For funded payouts, 5 qualifying trading days are required between payout requests.

faq.q: Is Apex Trader Funding better than Topstep?
faq.a: Both firms are competitive futures prop firms. Apex scored 74/100 in our 2026 review, with strengths in one-time evaluation fees, 100% payout split, and international availability. The best choice depends on your specific needs — compare drawdown models, payout rules, and platform preferences.
